channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Review of Murray-Darling Basin Plan Seeks Local Community Input
item.description: The Murray-Darling Basin, covering over one million square kilometres and home to 2.4 million people, is a cornerstone of Australia's agriculture and environmental health. Its waterways support about ...

item.title: Opera GX Gaming Browser Launches Native Support for Debian, Ubuntu, Fedora, and openSUSE
item.description: Opera GX, a gaming-focused web browser developed by Opera, has officially launched for Linux distributions including Debian, Ubuntu, Fedora, and openSUSE-based systems. Announced on March 20, 2026, vi...

item.title: Tanzania Reaffirms Commitment to Safe and Voluntary Return of Burundian Refugees
item.description: Tanzania has once again emphasized its commitment to ensuring the voluntary, safe, and dignified return of Burundian refugees in line with national and international laws. Mr. Sudi Mwakibasi, Director...

item.title: Premium Petrol Prices Increase by Rs 2/Litre Amid Rising Global Oil Prices
item.description: In India, the price of premium petrol has increased by Rs 2 per litre due to a rise in global oil prices, primarily triggered by the ongoing conflict in West Asia. While premium petrol now costs Rs 10...

item.title: Nyeri Teacher Receives Honest and Funny Notes from Form 3 Learners About School and Life
item.description: In Karatina, Nyeri county, Teacher Wambui recently asked her Form 3 learners to write anonymous notes about anything troubling them academically or personally. The exercise revealed a mix of funny, to...

item.title: Two people arrested for attempting to enter Faslane naval base in Scotland
item.description: Police Scotland reported that a man and a woman were detained after trying to gain access to HM Naval Base Clyde, also known as Faslane, on Thursday evening around 5 p.m. The base, located on the west...

item.title: How Certain Evangelical Beliefs Influence Views on Trump and End Times
item.description: Recent reports reveal that over 100 complaints have been filed with the Military Religious Freedom Foundation (MRFF) by U.S. service members, alleging that some military commanders are framing the U.S...

item.title: Fact-check: Kenyan Finance Minister’s Claims on Debt, Education Spending and Power Needs
item.description: Kenya’s finance minister, John Mbadi, has defended the newly passed National Infrastructure Fund Act, saying it will allow the government to fund projects like ports, roads and power expansion without...

item.title: Bishop Defends Public Muslim Prayers in Trafalgar Square Amid Political Criticism
item.description: Arun Arora, bishop of Kirkstall in the Diocese of Leeds, addresses the recent political criticism surrounding an open iftar held in Trafalgar Square. He draws a parallel with established Christian pub...

item.title: Pakistan's Rapid Solar Adoption Provides Buffer Against Middle East Energy Disruptions
item.description: Pakistan is experiencing a significant solar power boom, with rooftop panels and distributed solar systems reducing reliance on the national grid and imported fuels. Energy expert Vaqar Zakaria has go...

item.title: Israeli airstrikes kill four in Gaza as Rafah crossing reopens for limited aid
item.description: On Thursday, Israeli airstrikes targeted Gaza City, resulting in the deaths of four Palestinians, according to local officials. The strikes hit Tuffah and Zeitoun neighbourhoods, with two bodies repor...

item.title: CBI Continues Interrogation of Anil Ambani in Rs 2,929 Crore Bank Fraud Investigation
item.description: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) has continued questioning Anil Ambani, the chairman of the Reliance Group, for the second consecutive day in connection with an alleged bank fraud case. The c...

item.title: Marcos administration urges unity and peace as Muslims celebrate Eid’l Fitr
item.description: The Philippine national government joined the Muslim community in celebrating Eid’l Fitr, marking the end of Ramadan with a renewed appeal for peace, unity, and faith among Filipinos. Special Assistan...

item.title: The Alibi: Stella’s Struggle to Protect Katherine and Joseph in Episode 5
item.description: In the latest episode of *The Alibi*, Stella faces her hardest challenge yet as she makes a significant sacrifice to ensure the safety and survival of Katherine and Joseph. With tension mounting, Stel...

item.title: Journalists in Mwanza Trained to Report on Plastic Pollution in Lake Victoria
item.description: Journalists and journalism students in Mwanza Region have been urged to take a leading role in raising public awareness about the growing problem of plastic pollution in Lake Victoria. Speaking during...

item.title: NCP car park operator enters administration amid rising debts and declining demand
item.description: National Car Parks (NCP), one of the UK’s largest car park operators, has entered administration with a debt burden of £305 million. Established in 1931, NCP once enjoyed prestige and played host to L...

item.title: Kenya's Strategy to Strengthen Africa’s Voice in Global Ocean Governance
item.description: Kenya is gearing up to host the first Our Ocean Conference (OOC) ever held in Africa, set for June 2026 in Mombasa. The event gives the country and the continent a powerful platform to shape global oc...

item.title: FIFA Introduces Rule Requiring Female Coach or Assistant in Women's Tournaments, Reaffirms 2026 World Cup Plans Amid Iran Concerns
item.description: The news article from NaijaNews reports that FIFA don introduce new rules wey require every team for women’s competitions to get at least one female as head coach or assistant coach. This one na to bo...

item.title: Details on the U.K.'s Meningitis Outbreak and Health Response
item.description: Health authorities in the U.K. are investigating a deadly meningitis outbreak linked to a nightclub in southeast England. At least 27 cases have been identified, with 15 confirmed as meningococcal dis...

item.title: Kenya Met Issues Advisory for Heavy Rains Affecting Most Regions from March 20 to 24
item.description: The Kenya Meteorological Department has issued a five-day weather advisory warning of heavy rainfall across most parts of the country from Friday, March 20, to Tuesday, March 24, 2026. The rains are e...
